name: Reel Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = a horizontally scrolling overflow strip. Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (overflow-inline: auto + flex row + scroll-snap-type) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

item-width.description: Each item's inline-size along the strip. Absent → the item's intrinsic width.

gap.description: Even spacing between items along the strip, on a spacing scale.

snap.description: Scroll-snap alignment for items as the strip scrolls. Default none (free scroll).

height.description: Whether the strip's height tracks its content (auto) or is fixed. Default auto.

description:
What This Is

A horizontally scrolling strip of items that overflows its container rather than wrapping, with optional scroll-snap — media carousels, horizontal card shelves, image strips.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — a horizontally scrolling overflow strip — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (overflow-inline: auto + flex row + scroll-snap-type) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • item-width: Each item's inline-size along the strip. Absent → the item's intrinsic width.
  • gap: Even spacing between items along the strip, on a spacing scale (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).
  • snap: Scroll-snap alignment for items as the strip scrolls. Default none (free scroll) (none · start · center).
  • height: Whether the strip's height tracks its content (auto) or is fixed. Default auto (auto · fixed).

Annotation, not identity

An optional region landmark applies when the reel is a named, navigable shelf — a content annotation. The earns-role test is met: a scrolling strip is a distinct arrangement, not a variant of cluster (which wraps).
